mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and economic activity, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows consistent growth but the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of such periods, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above a certain threshold signals potential erosion of corporate profit margins and increases the likelihood of monetary policy tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 4% year-over-year, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling unemployment (UNRATE) and strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates a tightening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by > 0.2pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS grows by > 100k in 3 months
failure_condition: If both UNRATE and PAYEMS conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust housing starts (HOUST) signal strong investment and consumer confidence in the economy, which typically precedes broader economic growth and positive equity market performance, especially during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: If HOUST shows consistent strength during recovery periods, but S&P 500 60-day returns are consistently negative (Rank IC < 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates an accommodative monetary policy, which reduces the cost of capital and encourages investment, supporting higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for a sustained period, but S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of those periods, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity and broader economic contraction, leading to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth falls below -2% annualized over 3 months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with robust retail sales (RSAFS) indicates broad-based economic expansion and strong consumer demand, which are fundamental drivers of corporate profitability and equity market appreciation over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth > 3% AND RSAFS year-over-year growth > 5%
failure_condition: If both GDPC1 and RSAFS conditions are met, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or flat (average return < 0.1%) over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and potential recession, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points in 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.
